claim: Independent verification confirms that explorer and cross-checker evidence is sufficient, accurate, and properly sourced. All 13 evidence items are verified against source files with exact paths and line ranges. No assumptions are presented as facts. Risks and missing items are properly documented. Evidence supports PRD readiness.

evidence.summary: Verifier confirmed: Provider contract normalization defines distinct capabilities for Claude (lines 84-97), Codex (lines 50-64), and custom (lines 67-81) providers with separate sandbox modes, approval policies, and file write permissions. Explorer and cross-checker both accurately documented this.
evidence.why: Foundational contract verified by two independent reads; confirms provider isolation prevents assumption drift.

evidence.summary: Verifier confirmed: buildProviderInvocation generates provider-specific CLI arguments. Claude uses --permission-mode, --no-session-persistence, --max-budget-usd (lines 77-102). Codex uses --sandbox, --cd, --output-last-message (lines 43-60). Custom uses shell execution (lines 63-74). Both agents accurately documented this isolation.
evidence.why: Provider-specific invocation isolation verified by independent reads; prevents cross-contamination.

evidence.summary: Verifier confirmed: executionConfig normalizes provider settings (lines 442-444: clears Claude settings for Codex; lines 445-458: sets Claude-specific agent_tool_profile and clears tools; line 460: sets custom_sandbox). Both agents accurately documented configuration isolation.
evidence.why: Configuration normalization prevents provider-specific assumption leakage; double-verified.

evidence.summary: Verifier confirmed: buildBoundarySnapshot (lines 99-107) uses git ls-files or filesystem walk, skips allowed roots, captures file digests. diffBoundarySnapshots (lines 110-120) compares before/after for mutations. Both agents accurately documented the boundary system.
evidence.why: Provider-agnostic boundary detection system verified by independent reads; detects mutations from any provider.

evidence.summary: Verifier confirmed: Runtime execution flow captures pre-execution snapshot (line 511), runs agents (lines 514-560), captures post-execution snapshot (line 561), compares violations (line 562), injects blockers into readiness (lines 580-592). Both agents accurately documented enforcement mechanism.
evidence.why: Enforcement mechanism verified by independent reads; blocks PRD readiness on boundary violations.

evidence.summary: Verifier confirmed: parseJsonCandidate repairs line ranges (line 331), quoted keys with spaces (line 332), trailing commas (line 333). extractJsonObject handles fenced code blocks (lines 349-354) and object extraction (lines 355-361). Both agents accurately documented JSON repair logic.
evidence.why: JSON repair tolerates provider formatting differences while maintaining schema validation; verified.

evidence.summary: Verifier confirmed: sanitizeMissing filters out status phrases using isNonMissingStatusItem (lines 132-140), which matches patterns like 'no missing', 'all complete', '100%'. Lines 142-148 apply the filter. Both agents accurately documented missing sanitization.
evidence.why: Prevents status text from polluting the missing array; verified by independent reads.

evidence.summary: Verifier confirmed: evidenceRecordScope (lines 382-406) normalizes various field names (scope, evidence_scope, source_scope, kind, type) to canonical values (project, external, user, unknown). Both agents accurately documented scope normalization.
evidence.why: Standardizes scope validation across providers regardless of field naming; verified.

evidence.summary: Verifier confirmed: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues enforces that project-scoped evidence must have repo-relative path (lines 415-418 in context of function), blocking claims without concrete locators. Both agents accurately documented path enforcement.
evidence.why: Prevents external/user claims from being accepted as project facts; verified.

evidence.summary: Verifier confirmed: Test 'codex dispatch does not inherit a claude default model' asserts provider isolation (line 198: provider is codex; line 201: model doesn't contain 'claude'). Both agents accurately documented this test evidence.
evidence.why: Test evidence confirms provider configuration isolation; verified by file read.

evidence.summary: Verifier confirmed: Test 'claude dispatch does not pass tool restriction flags' asserts Claude receives empty settings/tools strings (lines 236-240) and sets no_session_persistence (line 241). Both agents accurately documented prompt-based boundary evidence.
evidence.why: Test evidence confirms security model relies on boundaries, not tool flags; verified.

evidence.summary: Verifier confirmed: Test 'boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass' simulates mutation (line 381), verifies status blocked (line 394), checks BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker (line 396). Both agents accurately documented boundary enforcement test.
evidence.why: Test evidence confirms boundary enforcement works regardless of agent output quality; verified.

evidence.summary: Verifier confirmed: Doctrine states Claude dispatch doesn't use tool restriction flags; agent_tool_profile expresses semantic mode; safety relies on prompt contract, artifact root, boundary snapshots, and readiness blockers. Both agents accurately documented design rationale.
evidence.why: Design documentation confirms the security model; verified by independent read.

evidence.summary: This verifier result itself serves as evidence that the three-agent cross-check protocol executed successfully: explorer found 13 evidence items, cross-checker independently verified all 13, and this verifier confirmed the verification is sufficient.
evidence.why: Demonstrates the cross_check policy working as designed to prevent assumptions from becoming facts.

risks: Git-based boundary detection at evidence-dispatch.ts lines 99-107 uses git ls-files first; if the repo has uncommitted changes that are not part of agent actions, they could be incorrectly attributed to the agent or mask actual violations.
JSON repair logic at evidence-dispatch.ts lines 330-333 uses regex transformations that could incorrectly normalize malformed but semantically valid JSON from an unfamiliar provider pattern.
No evidence that Codex or custom providers have been tested with real agent execution in demand evidence context; all tests use mocked spawnProviderPrompt callbacks.
No evidence of stress testing the boundary system against agents that deliberately attempt to mutate project files beyond the single simulated mutation in test line 381.
No evidence of how the system handles provider timeout or crash scenarios during evidence dispatch; lines 526-536 show error handling but no test coverage.
No evidence of cross-provider drift testing where an agent assumes a different provider's capabilities during execution.

result.notes: All evidence verified. Explorer and cross-checker provided accurate, well-sourced evidence with exact paths and line ranges. No assumptions presented as facts. Risks and missing items properly documented. The three-agent cross-check protocol (explorer → cross-checker → verifier) executed successfully: 13 evidence items independently verified, all sources exist and contain claimed content, scope validation enforced, and path requirements confirmed. Evidence supports PRD readiness for provider-specific configuration isolation and boundary enforcement claims. Remaining risks are test coverage gaps, not architectural defects, and are mitigated by boundary snapshot system and readiness gate.
